demographics.living_situation: lives at home with spouse; house sits at the top of a steep incline

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Every time I walk up the slight hill to my house, I get this heavy squeezing in my chest that goes down my left arm, but it completely disappears if I stop and rest for five minutes. My wife is really worried it's little heart attacks.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It's been happening for about a month whenever I walk up the hill and it's making me and my wife anxious — we want to know if it's my heart.

symptom_narrative.duration: each episode lasts until I stop and rest, then clears in about 5 to 10 minutes
symptom_narrative.character: a heavy squeezing or pressing feeling, like a weight on my chest
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: enough to make me stop walking; it frightens me but it isn't unbearable
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: middle of my chest, and it travels down the inside of my left arm
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: only when I exert myself — pretty much every time I walk up the hill, about the same distance each time (a couple of hundred metres up the slope)
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: stopping and standing still to rest — it goes completely within five to ten minutes
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: walking up the hill, and it seems worse when it's cold outside
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: I get a bit short of breath at the same time as the chest tightness, but that settles when the chest settles
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: nothing like this before the last month
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I've started avoiding the hill or walking really slowly so I don't set it off

past_medical_history.how_managed: takes a daily tablet for it

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with spouse who is present and anxious; home is on a steep hill that he has to walk up daily

objective_data_offered.value: spouse says he looks completely his normal self right now — comfortable, breathing normally, not sweating, chatting fine while sitting
objective_data_offered.timing: now, at rest during the call

objective_data_offered.value: he says he has no chest pain at all at the moment while sitting still
